name: Micronesia

visa_free.notes: US citizens may enter FSM to live, work, or study indefinitely without visas or non-citizen registration requirements per the Compact of Free Association (Section 142(a)). No statutory limit on length of stay; entry permit may be issued for up to one year at a time, renewable. US passport must be valid for at least 180 days from date of entry per travel.state.gov guidance.

Under the Agreement between the Federated States of Micronesia and the European Union on the short-stay visa waiver, signed 19 September 2016 (entered into provisional application 20 September 2016), citizens of EU member states — excluding Ireland, which opted out per protocols annexed to the EU treaties — may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. UK citizens are also excluded following Brexit. Applies to holders of valid ordinary, diplomatic, service/official, or special passports issued by EU member states. Excludes travel for the purpose of carrying out a paid activity. Formalised by Council Decision (EU) 2016/1879 and Council Decision (EU) 2017/234. The UK opted out pre-Brexit and remains excluded post-Brexit.

Citizens and nationals of Palau may enter visa-free for up to 30 days without an entry permit. They may prove citizenship by birth certificate or FSM entry permit (same as FSM and Marshall Islands citizens). An entry permit may be issued for the duration of the visit not exceeding one year (compared with 90 days maximum for general nationalities).

Citizens and nationals of the Marshall Islands may enter visa-free for up to 30 days without an entry permit. They may prove citizenship by birth certificate or FSM entry permit. An entry permit may be issued for the duration of the visit not exceeding one year (compared with 90 days maximum for general nationalities).

visa_policy.notes: FSM operates a de-facto visa-free policy for all nationalities for stays up to 30 days (extendable by entry permit up to 90 days total for general nationalities; up to 1 year for US/Palau/Marshall Islands citizens). US citizens have unlimited stay rights under the Compact of Free Association (no fixed maximum, permit renewable up to one year at a time). EU member state citizens (excluding Ireland and UK) have an enhanced 90-day entitlement per 180-day period under the 2016 bilateral visa waiver agreement — all passport types covered. No e-visa or ETA system exists. No visa-on-arrival fee — entry is free of visa charges for all nationalities.

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Israel holding valid diplomatic or service passports are exempted from the obligation to obtain a visa for the purpose of entering FSM for a visit or stay not exceeding 90 days within six (6) months from the date of first entry. Nationals intending to stay beyond 90 days must obtain visas in advance. Nationals exercising official functions as diplomatic/consular staff and their household family members are not required to obtain a visa within 30 days of entry; visas for the full period of assignment are required thereafter. Signed in Jerusalem on 21 January 2010 (6th of Shvat 5770).

cbi.notes: No citizenship by investment programme exists. FSM Constitution prohibits dual citizenship. Naturalisation is possible after 5 years of lawful residence under Article III of the FSM Constitution but this is not an investment programme. No official CBI programme was found on any FSM government source.

agreements: Compact of Free Association with the United States (freedom of movement for US citizens into FSM indefinitely; FSM citizens into USA)

Agreement between the Federated States of Micronesia and the European Union on the Short-Stay Visa Waiver, signed 19 September 2016, provisional application from 20 September 2016 (all passport types — ordinary, diplomatic, service/official, special; 90 days per 180-day period; excludes Ireland and UK; excludes paid activity)

Agreement between the Government of the Federated States of Micronesia and the Government of the State of Israel on Visa Exemption for Holders of Diplomatic and Service Passports, signed 21 January 2010

validation_notes: CORRECTED: EU visa waiver signing date clarified to 19 September 2016 (signed) with provisional application from 20 September 2016. Previous record listed only '20 September 2016' without distinguishing signing from provisional application date.

CORRECTED: EU visa waiver notes now explicitly state UK citizens are excluded (previously only stated 'excluding UK and Ireland' in passing; now clarified that the UK opted out pre-Brexit and remains excluded post-Brexit).

CORRECTED: EU entry source_url updated from the EEAS press page to the authoritative EUR-Lex legal text (CELEX:22016A1025(01)) which was verified to contain the full agreement text confirming all passport types and terms.

CORRECTED: Palau (PLW) and Marshall Islands (MHL) entry notes updated to clarify that their max_stay_days=30 is the initial visa-free period without an entry permit; they can obtain entry permits valid up to 1 year (vs 90-day max for general nationalities). The distinction between their privileged permit duration and the general 90-day cap is now explicit.
CORRECTED: US citizen notes now specify the 180-day passport validity requirement per travel.state.gov, which was missing from the prior record.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: The general 30-day visa-free regime for all nationalities is correct, extendable to maximum 90 days total via entry permit — confirmed by the FSM Embassy entry permit application page and FAQ.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: No visa-on-arrival, e-visa, or ETA system exists.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: Israel conditional access is limited to diplomatic and service passport holders — confirmed by the 2010 agreement referenced in the document list.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: No credential-based entry rules found (no rules granting entry based on holding a valid US visa, Schengen visa, green card, OCI card, or any other third-country credential).

NOTE: The EEAS source (eeas.europa.eu/node/10123_en) mentions FSM joins 7 other Pacific nations with similar EU waiver arrangements: Marshall Islands, Vanuatu, Samoa, Tonga, Palau, Kiribati, and Tuvalu. This confirms these Pacific nations also have separate EU visa waivers but those pertain to outbound travel, not FSM inbound.
gaps: The Costa Rica Visa Waiver PDF at fsmembassy.fm/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/CostaRica_VisaWaiver.pdf is a scanned image and not machine-readable; passport types covered (ordinary vs. diplomatic/service only) and exact terms cannot be extracted. Cannot confirm whether this extends to ordinary passports of Costa Rican nationals or only diplomatic/service passport holders.

EFTA Schengen-area countries (Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland): Wikipedia's 'Visa policy of Micronesia' article lists these as getting 90 days, but the official EU-FSM agreement text on EUR-Lex and EEAS only references EU member states. No separate FSM-EFTA bilateral agreement was found on official FSM sources. Whether EFTA Schengen associates are covered by FSM's general 30-day regime or an extended 90-day arrangement remains unconfirmed from official FSM government sources.

Ireland: Explicitly excluded from the EU-FSM visa waiver. UK citizens are also excluded. Both fall under the general 30-day visa-free regime. No separate bilateral agreement covering ordinary Irish or UK passports was found.

No official FSM government page enumerates a country-by-country visa exemption list beyond what is stated above. The 'all nationalities 30-day visa-free' rule is stated on the FSM Embassy site without enumerating individual countries.
No information found on any official FSM source regarding RBI, fast-track, skilled worker, startup, or digital nomad visa programmes.
The FSM does not appear to have any dedicated e-visa or ETA portal; no official e-visa system found.
No credential-based conditional entry rules found; the FSM Immigration Regulations confirm entry is based on nationality/citizenship, not possession of third-country visas or permits.
Mexico visa waiver document on fsmembassy.fm (M_DOJ_Visa_Waive.pdf) concerns FSM citizens travelling TO Mexico (not entry INTO FSM); not relevant to inbound conditional access.

The FSM Immigration Regulations PDF (RevFSMImmigrationReg.pdf) is an image-encoded scan and could not be parsed for full regulatory text confirming permit extension limits or prohibited-nationality lists.

outbound_official.note: No official Micronesia government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The FSM Embassy in Washington D.C. (fsmembassydc.org) maintained a page titled 'Visas for FSM Citizens' (http://www.fsmembassydc.org/page/visas-for-fsm-citizens) that is the closest known official origin-side resource, but the domain is currently offline (ECONNREFUSED as of 2026-06-14). The FSM Embassy in Honolulu (fsmembassy.fm/forms-and-documents-2/) hosts scanned-image PDFs of individual bilateral visa waiver agreements for FSM citizens travelling to Costa Rica, EU member states, and Mexico, and references a 'List of Countries and Visa Requirements' document without a direct link. The FSM Department of Foreign Affairs (dfa.gov.fm) is inaccessible due to a TLS certificate error. No page on gov.fm or fsmgov.org enumerates outbound visa-free destinations. The FSM Embassy Tokyo (fsmemb.or.jp) contains no outbound travel visa information.
